subject: Anthropology
title: Value Theory
description: A quick, practical guide to Value Theory.

hook.text: A diamond costs more than water, but water keeps you alive. Why do we value rare things over essential ones?

buildup.text: Anthropologists ask what makes things valuable. Is it labor, scarcity, demand, or social meaning that creates value?

discovery.text: Marx said value comes from labor. Simmel said it comes from desire and distance. Each theory reveals different truths.

twist.text: Value isn't just economic. A family heirloom is worthless at auction but priceless to its owner. Context creates value.

climax.text: Value is always relational. Nothing has value in isolation — only in a web of social relationships and cultural meaning.

punchline.text: Value is created by people, not inherent in things.

quiz.question: Where does value come from according to anthropology?
options: From social relationships, labor, desire, and cultural meaning
